own_review.issue: Unguarded NPE: new condition `shell != ideShell && shell.getData().getClass().getName().startsWith(WIZARD_CLASSNAME_TEMPLATE)` dereferences shell.getData() with no null check. This runs inside a global display.addFilter(SWT.Paint, ...) that fires for every painted Composite on the display; shells with no data set (tooltips, hovers, content-assist popups, progress shells) return null from getData(), throwing NPE inside the SWT event loop during the test. Needs `shell.getData() != null &&` guard.

The guard matches on internal Eclipse class-name prefix 'org.eclipse.ltk.internal.ui.refactoring.Refactoring' (internal LTK API, no LTK references elsewhere in the repo). Brittle across Eclipse releases; additionally, the 'Refactoring'-titled error dialog branch may become unreachable if that dialog is a plain JFace MessageDialog whose data class does not match the LTK prefix (uncertain, not independently verifiable from this repo).

`Set<String> buttons = getButtons(c)` now performs a full recursive widget-tree traversal with HashSet allocations on every Paint event before knowing whether the result is needed; the old hasButton short-circuited and was only called inside the relevant branches. Negligible for a test but strictly worse per-event work.
Sleep bumped 2000ms->5000ms is a timing band-aid; adds 3s to every run (longest fixed sleep in the suite; TestAngular's 4000ms was previously the max). Polling for LS readiness would be more robust.
download-maven-plugin 1.6.1->1.6.7 bump is unrelated to the PR's stated purpose (test fix); scope creep, undocumented rationale.
`static private` modifier order is unconventional (`private static`); constant name WIZARD_CLASSNAME_TEMPLATE is misleading — it is a class-name prefix, not a template.

points.note: Unguarded shell.getData() NPE inside global SWT.Paint filter — the known real blocker; correct mechanism (filter fires for all widgets, data-less shells) and correct fix.
Sleep 2000->5000 CI-time observation. The cross-suite claim ('next longest is 4000ms in TestAngular') was verified TRUE by grep, so accurate — but the point itself is a non-defect timing nit.
Internal LTK API class-name prefix fragility — real, verified (org.eclipse.ltk.internal.ui.refactoring is internal).
Unrelated version bump / scope-creep observation — accurate but process nit.
`static private` ordering nit — accurate but style-only.

exceeds: Found the shell.getData() NPE blocker (not in human reference)
Verified cross-suite sleep comparison (TestAngular 4000ms) — checked true against the repo

points.note: Unguarded shell.getData() NPE — the known real blocker; strongest writeup: notes the listener path has no try-catch unlike pressOk/pressCancel, gives exact fix.
Internal LTK API via string; absence claim 'no LTK references exist anywhere in this repo' verified TRUE by repo-wide grep.

Version bump rationale nit. Its '6 modules / VSCode, Chrome debug adapter, ESLint LS, Node.js archives' claim verified TRUE (6 pom files; fetch-vscode, fetch-chrome-debug-adapter, fetch-eslint-ls executions plus node embedders) — accurate but the point is a process nit.
Sleep-is-heuristic observation with poll-for-readiness suggestion; reasonable, non-defect.
getButtons HashSet-per-recursion allocation; accurate and self-labeled cosmetic. Misses the stronger angle (unconditional traversal on every Paint event), but harmless.

exceeds: Found the shell.getData() NPE blocker (not in human reference)
Repo-wide verified absence of LTK references supporting the internal-API point
Enumerated download-maven-plugin usage across modules — checked true against the repo

qualitative: Both candidates found the one real blocker — the unguarded shell.getData() NPE inside the global SWT.Paint filter (TestJsTs.java:111) — with correct mechanism and fix; v6's writeup is marginally stronger (contrasts with the try-catch protection in pressOk/pressCancel). The human reference point targets META-INF/MANIFEST.MF, a file not present in the provided diff, so it was excluded from missed-point scoring. Every verifiable factual claim in both reviews checked out against the repo: v5's 'next-longest sleep is 4000ms in TestAngular' is true, and v6's 'no LTK references anywhere in this repo' and '6 modules use download-maven-plugin' are both true — zero absence-fabrications on either side. Beyond the blocker, both add one solid verified point (internal LTK API fragility) plus accurate but trivial nits (sleep bump, pom scope creep, style/allocation), landing them in a dead heat at net +3 each. v6 shows slightly deeper repo grounding from its git tools, but it did not translate into additional scored findings.
